hints.text: Plates white or **yellow** (in S. America yellow = only Peru/Colombia); taxis = yellow top / white bottom
Gen3 solid **black or white** Google car; **has Gen4** (Bolivia doesn't; Ecuador Gen4 = big pickup); smallcam exists
White outer lines; highways single-dashed/double yellow; **rural mountain roads have no centre line** (mostly Peru in SA)
**Black-and-white striped signposts** (only Peru in S. America); metal signposts have **concrete blocks at the base**
Bollards red or yellow, mostly **triangular concrete**; guardrails black/yellow striped (Peru's outer stripe black; Bolivia yellow)
km markers = **concrete, black-and-white**; **bridges commonly painted orange**
**Mototaxis** (tuk-tuks) common in cities; red-and-white stickers on taxis; rectangular metal electricity counters (2–3 slits)
Currency **Sol (S/.)**; many country-unique political-party logos painted on walls
Coverage mostly west + main highways; **flat east, mountainous west**; dry desert west, rainforest east
Highways numbered **north→south** (mostly west); 1 / 3 / 5 longest, cross multiple departments
Pole markings: **yellow vertical numbers** + a 2–3 letter city code (e.g. CH = Chimbote) → north coast & Tacna
**Red brick + blue-glass buildings** → near Lake Titicaca (like W. Bolivia); overhanging wood-supported roofs → north
**Lima** lamp posts have a sweeping **inverted-J** top; cylindrical black/yellow-top bollard = Moquegua (south)
